$comment: Normatively defined in AIP spec §11.1. Endorsement signature covers canonical field order: (endorsement_id, from_aid, to_aid, task_id, outcome, notes, timestamp, signature) with signature set to '' during signing. Self-endorsement (from_aid == to_aid) MUST be rejected by the Registry at the application layer — JSON Schema cannot assert cross-field inequality.
title: AIP Endorsement
description: A signed task-outcome record submitted by one AIP agent to endorse another. Defined in AIP spec §11.1. Endorsements feed the Reputation system — they record the outcome of a task that from_aid observed to_aid perform. The Registry stores these immutably. Self-endorsement (from_aid == to_aid) is prohibited and MUST be rejected by the Registry.

endorsement_id.description: Unique identifier for this endorsement. Format convention: 'end:' prefix followed by a UUID v4. Consistent with manifest_id ('cm:') and revocation_id ('rev:') prefix conventions.

from_aid.description: AID of the endorsing agent. MUST be registered and not revoked. MUST NOT equal to_aid (self-endorsement is prohibited by AIP spec §11.1 and rejected by the Registry).

to_aid.description: AID of the agent being endorsed. MUST be registered. MUST NOT equal from_aid.

task_id.description: Opaque task identifier linking this endorsement to a specific work unit. SHOULD match a task_id used in a delegation chain. Not validated by the Registry beyond being non-empty — the meaning is defined by the application layer.

outcome.description: Machine-readable task outcome. success — the task was completed as specified. partial — the task was partially completed (e.g. time-bounded ephemeral agent, incomplete result). failure — the task failed or was not completed satisfactorily.

notes.description: OPTIONAL. Free-form human-readable context about the outcome. Not machine-enforced. Null or absent when no additional context is needed. Maximum 512 characters.

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of when this endorsement was issued. MUST be a valid RFC 3339 datetime. The Registry stores this as-is; no clock skew validation is enforced on endorsements.

signature.description: Base64url-encoded EdDSA signature of this endorsement, signed with from_aid's current private key. The Registry MUST verify this signature against the current public key of from_aid before recording. Invalid signatures MUST be rejected.

reputation_effect.$comment: Endorsements affect the to_aid reputation score in the Registry's reputation table. The aggregation algorithm (AIP spec §11.2) weights endorsements by the from_aid's own reputation score, recency, and outcome severity. The Registry MAY apply a minimum from_aid reputation threshold before counting endorsements.
